title: Zeuxine benguetensis
intro: Zeuxine benguetensis, also known as Benguet orchid or Philippine orchid, is a terrestrial orchid species native to the Philippines, characterized by its small, yellowish-green flowers and egg-shaped pseudobulbs. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Zeuxine benguetensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Zeuxine benguetensis is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as tinctures and ointments.
taxonomy: Zeuxine benguetensis is a species of orchid in the genus Zeuxine. This species is classified under the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. The genus Zeuxine belongs to the subfamily Orchidoideae. The subfamily Orchidoideae is part of the subfamily Orchidaceae which is one of the two main subfamilies in the orchid family. These classifications are based on the plant's morphology and molecular characteristics.

Zeuxine benguetensis has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
